    "R1.2": "The causal claim that structural markers—not the additional tokens they introduce—drive improvement rests solely on a three-ordering prompt ablation; the logical chain from data to conclusion is thin and requires verification.",
    "R1.3": "Assertions such as 'clearly outperforms' and 'advances the state of the art' are applied to gains of 0.6–1.5 pp over three baselines that are all author-constructed variants of the authors' own prior method; evidence-claim alignment is the single most load-bearing concern in this artifact.",
    "R1.4": "The novelty frame ('state of the art', 'superior elicitation strategy') is structurally unsupported without comparison to independent external baselines (e.g., Wei et al. 2022 standard CoT, Kojima et al. 2022 zero-shot CoT); the delta against nearest independent prior work is entirely unestablished.",

rationale.R1.2: The causal claim that structural markers—not the additional tokens they introduce—drive improvement rests solely on a three-ordering prompt ablation; the logical chain from data to conclusion is thin and requires verification.
rationale.R1.3: Assertions such as 'clearly outperforms' and 'advances the state of the art' are applied to gains of 0.6–1.5 pp over three baselines that are all author-constructed variants of the authors' own prior method; evidence-claim alignment is the single most load-bearing concern in this artifact.
rationale.R1.4: The novelty frame ('state of the art', 'superior elicitation strategy') is structurally unsupported without comparison to independent external baselines (e.g., Wei et al. 2022 standard CoT, Kojima et al. 2022 zero-shot CoT); the delta against nearest independent prior work is entirely unestablished.
rationale.R1.7: All three baselines are anonymous self-citations; foundational CoT literature is absent from the framing; this gap materially undermines the scholarly positioning and novelty claim.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I review this for scientific rigor (claim validity, baseline adequacy, novelty framing) or for writing quality (prose clarity and structure)?
